name: La Foradada by the Bosque del Burgar - Circular from Sant Carles de la Ràpita
name_original: La Foradada por el Bosque del Burgar - Circular desde Sant Carles de la Ràpita
region_intro: Dog friendly hiking trails in the Ebro Delta traverse Catalonia's largest wetland, a unique landscape shaped by the Ebro River's processes. The region features vast rice fields that create a mosaic changing with the seasons, extensive lagoons, and long sandy beaches with coastal sand dunes. This flat, horizontal terrain offers diverse environments for outdoor activities. Best dog friendly hiking trails in the Ebro Delta The most popular dog friendly hiking route is Riumar Beach – Old Campsite by the Beach loop from Riumar, a 4.8 miles (7.8 km) trail that takes 1 hour 57 minutes to complete. This easy route follows the coastline, offering views of the beach. Another top favourite among local hikers is Riumar Beach – Garxal Observation Tower loop from Tamariu, a moderate 6.4 miles (10.3 km) path. This route leads through coastal areas towards an observation tower, providing views of the wetlands. Local hikers also love the Patrau Observation Tower – The Wooden House loop from Casa de Fusta, a 8.6 miles (13.8 km) trail leading through wetland landscapes, often completed in about 3 hours 27 minutes. Hiking in the Ebro Delta is defined by vast rice fields, extensive lagoons, and sandy beaches, offering unique coastal paths and wetland exploration. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy family-friendly routes to more challenging options. The routes in Ebro Delta are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.4 stars from over 600 reviews. More than 4000 hikers have used komoot to explore Ebro Delta's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many dog-friendly hiking trails are available in the Ebro Delta?
A: The Ebro Delta offers a wide selection of dog-friendly hiking trails. Our guide features 17 routes specifically curated for you and your canine companion, ranging from easy strolls to more moderate adventures.

Q: What kind of scenery can I expect on dog-friendly hikes in the Ebro Delta?
A: You and your dog can explore a unique 'sublimely horizontal landscape' characterized by vast rice fields that change appearance with the seasons, extensive lagoons, and long sandy beaches. Many trails traverse these iconic rice paddies and coastal paths, offering tranquil views and opportunities for birdwatching. The region is known for its extraordinary biodiversity, making every walk a chance to experience nature.

Q: Are there easy dog-friendly trails suitable for beginners or families?
A: Yes, the Ebro Delta has plenty of easy, dog-friendly routes perfect for beginners or families. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/23758939" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mirador del Través – The Wooden House loop from Casa de Fusta&lt;/a&gt; is a short, easy walk. Another great option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/23759377" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;The Wooden House – Mirador del Través loop from Casa de Fusta&lt;/a&gt;, which is also easy and offers a pleasant experience for all.

Q: Can I find circular dog-friendly routes in the Ebro Delta?
A: Absolutely! Many of the dog-friendly trails in the Ebro Delta are circular, allowing you to start and end at the same point. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/14889799" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Riumar Beach – Old Campsite by the Beach loop from Riumar&lt;/a&gt; is an excellent circular option, offering coastal views. Another popular choice is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/23760053" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Patrau Observation Tower – The Wooden House loop from Casa de Fusta&lt;/a&gt;, which takes you through diverse landscapes.

Q: Are there any dog-friendly trails that lead to beaches or water features?
A: Yes, the Ebro Delta is famous for its coastal areas and wetlands. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/10419891" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Riumar Beach – Garxal Observation Tower loop from Tamariu&lt;/a&gt; is a fantastic moderate trail that takes you along the beach and near the Garxal lagoon, offering beautiful views and a chance for your dog to enjoy the coastal environment. Many trails also pass by the region's extensive lagoons.

Q: What are some interesting landmarks or attractions I can visit with my dog in the Ebro Delta?
A: While specific access rules for dogs at all attractions may vary, many trails offer views of iconic landmarks. You can hike towards the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/802480" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Fangar Lighthouse&lt;/a&gt;, a popular destination across a sandy landscape. The region also features numerous viewpoints like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4121762" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Flamingo Viewing Area, Parc Natural del Delta de l'Ebre&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/979695" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Tancada Birdwatching Hide&lt;/a&gt;, which you can often enjoy from nearby trails with your dog.

Q: What is the best time of year to hike with my dog in the Ebro Delta?
A: The Ebro Delta experiences hot, dry summers and mild winters. Autumn and winter are ideal for hiking with your dog, as the temperatures are more comfortable and it's a prime time for birdwatching. Spring also offers pleasant conditions with the rice fields creating a 'Land of Mirrors' effect. It's best to avoid the peak summer heat for longer hikes with your dog.

Q: What should I pack for a dog-friendly hike in the Ebro Delta?
A: Given the flat and often exposed terrain, it's crucial to pack plenty of water for both yourself and your dog, especially during warmer months. Sun protection (hat, sunscreen) is also recommended. Don't forget a leash, waste bags, and any necessary dog first-aid supplies. Consider dog booties for sandy or rougher paths if your dog is sensitive.

Q: Are there any challenging dog-friendly hikes in the Ebro Delta?
A: While the Ebro Delta is largely flat, offering many easy and moderate routes, some trails can be longer or more exposed, making them more challenging. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/10419891" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Riumar Beach – Garxal Observation Tower loop from Tamariu&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate route that covers over 10 km, providing a good workout. Always check the route details for distance and duration to match your and your dog's fitness levels.

Q: What do other hikers enjoy most about dog-friendly trails in the Ebro Delta?
A: The Ebro Delta is highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.4 stars. Reviewers often praise the unique, flat landscapes, the tranquility of the rice fields and lagoons, and the opportunity to enjoy nature with their dogs. The well-maintained paths and diverse birdlife are frequently highlighted as favorite aspects of hiking in the region.

Q: Are there any short dog-friendly walks for a quick outing?
A: Yes, if you're looking for a shorter walk, the Ebro Delta has options. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/23758939" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mirador del Través – The Wooden House loop from Casa de Fusta&lt;/a&gt; is an easy, short route perfect for a quick stroll with your dog, covering just over 3.5 km. These shorter trails are great for a relaxed outing or for dogs with less stamina.
tips: Old farmhouse located under the Mata-redona mill, forming one of the only flats in the Montsià mountain range.

The direct route (sign: white line) is very steep with a few short unsecured and partly exposed climbing passages. Insect repellent is recommended below. At the top there is a great view over the Ebro Delta and along the coast to Peniscola as a reward.
The somewhat strenuous ascent, including a short climb, is rewarded with a magnificent view of the Ebro Delta!
